Mobilization Matrix

Joshua Project data at the people-group-across-countries (PGAC) level. Senders are chosen from all groups in a country; matching is scored PGAC-to-PGAC. Geography, risk, income, blocs and migrant corridors use each group's largest-population country. Religion is a significant but soft factor; distance is one modest signal; target-country risk is shown as a flag, not scored into the match.

Rank best mobilization matches

Pick a sender (any group in any country). The top 20 target peoples update automatically. Open a row to score it in detail.

Diaspora and "general" catch-all groups, and Jewish groups, are always down-ranked as targets.

Select the two groups

A is the sender (any group in any country). B is the target people (across countries).

How prefill and blanks work

Auto-scored from data with a green note: religion, language family, geographic distance, settlement pattern, sender church strength (Joshua Project); persecution (Open Doors WWL); security (Fragile States Index); government restriction (Pew GRI); habitat/biome (Koppen); diaspora corridor (UN DESA); plus income band and bloc ties. Factors with no loaded source stay blank and highlighted. Click any 0-5 to override. Blank factors are excluded and weights renormalize.

Data signals for this pair

Select both groups.

Domain weights

Balanced defaults. Weights renormalize across domains with data.

Score the factors

Green = from data. Orange = needs input.

Sender Readiness (Group A only)

Group A capacity to send. Sets the multiplier.
